_sources.ovx: CBOE CDN OVX_History.csv - D0 uses Feb 27 close (Feb 28 = Saturday); D7/D8 carry D6 (weekend, market closed); D10 live via /api/cboe
_sources.vxeem: CBOE CDN VXEEM_History.csv - all D0-D9 confirmed from live data; D7/D8 carry D6 (weekend); D10 live via /api/cboe
_sources.scenarios: D10-D12 confirmed from banker-conflict.json; all prior days estimated from narrative context

days.note: Wed - Brent $79.45 (TradingEconomics Jun 17, +0.6% minor bounce; -19% from D93 $98.57 peak). WTI $75.47; TTF €41.12 (-1.6%). At three-month lows. 2 days to MOU signing. Iran differing MOU versions + nuclear sequencing unresolved; deputy FM frozen-funds condition. Deal fragile but signing track intact. Tail 15%.

Thu - MOU digitally pre-signed at G7 Versailles (Jun 18, Haaretz). Formal ceremony at Burgenstock tomorrow. Brent $79.34 (TradingEconomics, -0.27%); WTI $76.43 (-0.47%); TTF €41.33 (-1.39%). Markets pricing deal confirmation. Tail 12%.

Fri - MOU formally signed Burgenstock, Switzerland — formal ceremony complete. Phase 2 nuclear talks begin immediately. US markets closed Juneteenth; Brent $79.34 carried (Jun 18 close). Escalation lowered to 2 (Elevated). Tail 10%.

Fri - First trading day after Juneteenth. Phase 2 nuclear talks in Switzerland abruptly postponed (CNBC). Iran disputes MOU text elements. Brent ~$79.80 est. (small rise on postponement uncertainty). Tail 11% (small reversal on postponement news).
Sat - Market closed; scenarios carried from D110.
Sun - US-Iran Phase 2 nuclear talks kick off in Switzerland (Al Jazeera). Vance leads US delegation. Oil futures open; Brent ~$79.25 (Fortune 8:45am ET). Roadmap talks positive signal. Tail 10%.

Mon - Vance-Iran first round concludes: 'roadmap toward final deal within 60 days' agreed (Al Jazeera/CNBC/NPR). IAEA inspectors 'invited' per Vance; Iran FM disputes. Lebanon deconfliction mechanism agreed. Brent ~$78.00 est. (-1.6%). Technical talks continue in Switzerland. Tail 10%.

Tue - Brent crashes -5.3% to $73.87 (Barchart implied) as tankers transit Hormuz; Goldman Sachs cuts Q4 Brent to $80 from $90 (Investing.com) — expects GCC exports normalised by end-Jul. WTI briefly below $70 (CNBC). Brent erasing wartime gains (Bloomberg). Tail 9%.

Wed - Brent $75.04 (TradingEconomics confirmed, +1.76% from $73.87 Jun 24 close). WTI ~$69.95 est. (below $70 Jun 25, CNBC). Partial rebound after steep sell-off. Gold $4,040.30 (TradingEconomics confirmed, +1.02%). Mine-clearing Day 6 of 30-day mandate. Technical talks continue. Tail 9%.

Thu - Brent ~$75.04 carried (Jun 25 confirmed; Jun 26 not yet confirmed). Roadmap agreed Jun 22-23; IAEA access disputed but Grossi says inspections 'will take place.' Mine-clearing Day 7 of 30-day mandate. Goldman Q4 $80 Brent. iTraxx ~128bp est. EM FX strengthening. Tail 8%.
